question: What happens when the following C++ statements execute?

answers.answer: Nothing is printed. A compile error occurs.
answers.feedback: Assigning a double to an int is legal in C++ (an implicit narrowing conversion); most compilers only warn about it.
day was declared as int, so it cannot hold the fractional part. C++ variables keep the type they were declared with.
Correct! Unlike Python, a C++ variable has a fixed type. Assigning 32.5 to an int truncates it to 32.
The assignment day = 32.5 replaces the old value; day holds the truncated result 32 when it is printed.
